title: Request Smuggling, Response Splitting, Parser Differentials, and Downgrades · Runtime Contract: Clte Tecl Defense
summary: A front end and origin disagree on length, whitespace, duplicate fields, target decoding, and line endings; the team tests each parser alone and forwards ambiguous messages after normalization. Planned artifact: Add and verify the clte tecl defense increment in multi-recipient parser-consistency threat model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and conflicting length signals, consume no ambiguous next message, and close the connection.
Continue HTTP/1.1 safely after a rejected transition only when buffered bytes, framing, peer behavior, and parser state remain unambiguous.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Clte Tecl Defense · Theory Clte Tecl Defense
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 2810 in a field-normalizing cache with an independent origin parser. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject framing and control-byte differentials before a message crosses recipients; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then minimize a fuzz failure and add it to the deterministic regression corpus. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Reject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and conflicting length signals, consume no ambiguous next message, and close the connection.
